challengeName: Building Web APIs with Modern Python Framework

questions.question: What interface standard does FastAPI implement to enable parallel request processing?
questions.explanation: FastAPI is an ASGI web framework that can leverage parallelism to process web requests, unlike Flask which uses WSGI.

questions.question: What underlying framework does FastAPI wrap to provide its functionality?
questions.explanation: FastAPI is effectively a wrapper over the Starlette framework, built by encode (the same team behind Django-rest-framework).

questions.question: What development pattern does FastAPI share with Flask regarding route creation?
questions.explanation: Both Flask and FastAPI allow creating routes by decorating functions with specialized decorators like @app.get('/').

questions.question: What specification format does FastAPI generate for API documentation?
questions.explanation: FastAPI generates an openapi.json specification of your web service following the OpenAPI standard.

questions.question: What ASGI server does FastAPI use to run the application?
questions.explanation: Uvicorn takes the FastAPI app object and starts the web server with decorated functions as API routes.

questions.question: What data format must Python objects be converted to before HTTP transmission?
questions.explanation: Data must be serialized from Python objects (like dictionaries) into JSON strings prior to HTTP transfer.

questions.question: What is the process called when browser converts received data back to usable objects?
questions.explanation: Deserialization converts JSON strings back into JavaScript objects by the browser after transmission completes.

questions.question: What pattern allows injecting utility functions into route handlers in FastAPI?
questions.explanation: FastAPI uses Inversion of Control where you inject dependencies into functions by passing them to Depends().

questions.question: What happens to dependency outputs during the lifetime of a request?
options: They are sent to external APIs
They are cached to prevent duplicate computation
questions.explanation: FastAPI caches dependency outputs at runtime during request processing to prevent duplicate computation.

questions.question: What is the default URL path to access auto-generated interactive API documentation?
questions.explanation: Access the auto-generated Swagger UI docs page at http://localhost:8000/docs by default.

questions.question: How does FastAPI handle both sync and async routes in the same application?
options: Requires separate servers for each type
Seamlessly runs async routes concurrently and sync routes synchronously
questions.explanation: FastAPI handles both sync (def) and async (async def) routes within the same application appropriately.

questions.question: For what type of operations are asynchronous functions particularly useful?
options: I/O-bound operations like database queries and GPU inference
questions.explanation: Async functions are useful for I/O-bound operations like querying databases or exchanging data with GPUs.

questions.question: What feature allows queuing long-running tasks without blocking the main response?
questions.explanation: Background Tasks allow handling long-running processes (like sending emails) without keeping clients waiting.

questions.question: What type of applications particularly benefit from background tasks in GenAI?
questions.explanation: Background tasks are useful for text-to-speech applications that need time to generate realistic audio outputs.

questions.question: What library does FastAPI use for data validation and serialization schemas?
questions.explanation: FastAPI uses Pydantic schemas for strict data validation, serialization, and type checking at runtime.

questions.question: What complex data types can Pydantic validators check at runtime?
options: Emails, URLs, UUIDs, and custom types
questions.explanation: Pydantic validators can validate complex data types at runtime like emails, URLs, UUIDs beyond static type checkers.

questions.question: What can you create by injecting dependencies into other dependencies?
options: Hierarchical dependency graph for complex business logic
questions.explanation: Dependencies can be injected into other dependencies creating hierarchical graphs for authentication and business logic.

questions.question: What are startup and shutdown events useful for in FastAPI applications?
options: Loading AI models into memory and cleanup
questions.explanation: Startup/shutdown events handle initialization like loading models and cleanup of temporary artifacts.

questions.question: What can middleware intercept in FastAPI?
options: All communications between client and service
questions.explanation: Middlewares intercept all communications between client and FastAPI service, exposing request and response objects.

questions.question: What common operations are middlewares used for?
options: Adding headers, authentication, logging, and monitoring
questions.explanation: Middlewares commonly add headers, perform auth checks, log communications, and monitor requests/responses.

questions.question: What does FastAPI's plugin ecosystem allow you to do?
questions.explanation: Plugins hook into FastAPI internals to extend functionality without dealing with integration order issues.

questions.question: What communication protocol does FastAPI support for real-time bidirectional communication?
questions.explanation: FastAPI supports WebSockets for maintaining persistent, bidirectional communication channels.

questions.question: What can Streaming Server Sent Events (SSE) be used for?
options: Streaming LLM outputs to clients in real-time
questions.explanation: SSE endpoints can stream LLM outputs to clients progressively as they are generated.

questions.question: Why is FastAPI's integration with modern Python beneficial?
options: IDE linters and formatters work natively with sensible defaults
questions.explanation: Since FastAPI uses modern Python, IDE linters and formatters can natively check and format code.

questions.question: What customization does FastAPI allow through Starlette base classes?
options: Creating custom classes for service layer customization
questions.explanation: FastAPI allows defining custom classes inheriting Starlette base classes to implement features per your preferences.

questions.question: What memory problem occurs when scaling FastAPI workers horizontally?
options: Memory usage decreases with more workers
Models use no memory at all
Memory is automatically shared between workers
Each worker loads a separate model instance into memory
questions.explanation: FastAPI doesn't share model memory between instances, requiring separate model loads per container.

questions.question: What Python limitation affects FastAPI even with asynchronous endpoints?
questions.explanation: Python's GIL means inference requests block the main event loop, preventing concurrent processing.

questions.question: Why can't FastAPI requests be efficiently batched for model inference?
options: No built-in support for micro-batch processing
questions.explanation: FastAPI doesn't support batching inference requests together for efficient parallel computation.

questions.question: What hardware separation problem does FastAPI have?
options: Cannot split requests between CPU and GPU processing
questions.explanation: FastAPI cannot split web requests between CPU (handles validation) and GPU (runs inference).

questions.question: What deployment challenges arise from ML model runtime coupling?
options: Dependency conflicts with native libraries and hardware
questions.explanation: Model runtimes are deeply coupled with native libraries and hardware, causing dependency conflicts.

questions.question: What type of AI workloads may require alternatives to FastAPI?
questions.explanation: For serving resource-intensive billion parameter models, consider alternatives like BentoML.

questions.question: What framework is specifically designed for ML workflows like FastAPI?
questions.explanation: BentoML is built on Starlette with FastAPI patterns specifically designed for machine learning.

questions.question: What advantage does BentoML have over FastAPI for AI workloads?
options: Scales web requests separately from model inference
questions.explanation: BentoML's architecture allows scaling web requests separately from model inference for flexibility.

questions.question: What architectural pattern does Django follow?
questions.explanation: Django expects you to adopt the Model View Controller (MVC) architecture for organizing code.

questions.question: What does Django ship with out of the box?
options: Admin panel, authentication system, ORM, and security features
questions.explanation: Django provides admin panel, credentials-based auth, ORM system, and security features out of the box.

questions.question: When was async request support added to Django?
questions.explanation: Django introduced support for async requests in version 4.2.

questions.question: What is Django's main limitation for modern API development?
options: Heavy application slowing development, deployment, and scaling
questions.explanation: Django results in heavy applications that slow down development, deployment, and scaling of services.

questions.question: What communication protocol does Flask use?
questions.explanation: Flask implements WSGI for web serving, processing requests synchronously.

questions.question: What is Flask's main limitation compared to ASGI frameworks?
options: Does not natively support WebSocket endpoints
questions.explanation: Because Flask implements WSGI, it does not natively support WebSocket endpoints.

questions.question: What features does Flask lack compared to FastAPI?
options: Data validation, auto-documentation, and dependency injection
questions.explanation: Flask doesn't come with data validation, auto-documentation, and dependency injection out of the box.

questions.question: What type of frameworks give too much freedom causing decision fatigue?
options: Unopinionated frameworks like FastAPI and Flask
questions.explanation: Non-opinionated frameworks like FastAPI give freedom but may cause decision fatigue when choosing packages.

questions.question: What architectural pattern is becoming more popular than monoliths?
questions.explanation: Microservice architectural patterns are becoming more popular as businesses separate backend and frontend.

questions.question: What is the recommended starting project structure for new FastAPI services?
questions.explanation: Start with a flat structure when the complexity of your system is not yet clear.

questions.question: What problem does nested structure cause when modifying code?
options: Too few files to work with
Shotgun updates requiring changes across many files
questions.explanation: Nested structure causes shotgun updates where changes cascade requiring many file modifications.

questions.question: What structure groups related modules by domain/feature?
questions.explanation: Modular structure groups closely interconnected modules referring to a specific domain together.

questions.question: What FastAPI project popularized the modular structure?
questions.explanation: The modular structure was popularized by the Netflix Dispatch FastAPI project.

questions.question: What is the innermost layer of the Onion architecture?
questions.explanation: The innermost layer contains domain models and business logic with outer layers depending inwardly.

questions.question: What principle states high-level modules should not depend on low-level implementations?
questions.explanation: Dependency Inversion Principle states high-level modules should declare needs from low-level modules via dependencies.

questions.question: What Python version is recommended for FastAPI projects?
questions.explanation: Python 3.11 is recommended as some deployment environments may not yet support latest versions.

questions.question: What must Django models be redefined as when migrating to FastAPI?
questions.explanation: When migrating from Django, redefine all serializer models using Pydantic for FastAPI serialization.
